title: Shingles Herbal Remedies: 10 Teas to Soothe and Heal
intro_desc: Herbal teas for shingles are a natural remedy that helps alleviate the symptoms of shingles, a painful and contagious rash caused by the reactivation of the varicella-zoster virus. The best herbal teas for shingles include Zingiber officinale, Echinacea purpurea, Aloe barbadensis, Hypericum perforatum, Melissa officinalis, Ginkgo biloba, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Valeriana officinalis, Vaccinium myrtillus, and Urtica dioica. If left untreated, shingles can significantly impact a person's quality of life, causing chronic pain, discomfort, and emotional distress, often resulting in isolation and decreased productivity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea has been used by some people to help with shingles. Shingles is a painful skin condition caused by the varicella-zoster virus, which also causes chickenpox. When this virus reactivates, it can cause a rash and severe pain on one side of the body. Some people believe that Echinacea purpurea tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and swelling associated with shingles. The tea is made from the flowers and roots of the Echinacea plant, which is native to North America. The active compounds in Echinacea purpurea, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, may help to boost the immune system and reduce the severity of shingles symptoms. Some studies suggest that Echinacea purpurea may also have antiviral properties, which could help to fight the varicella-zoster virus. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Echinacea purpurea tea for shingles.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Polyphenols are good for shingles because they have strong anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the pain and swelling associated with shingles, and may also help to reduce the severity and duration of the condition.
Flavonoids have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the pain and swelling associated with shingles by blocking the production of certain chemicals that cause inflammation.
Alkylamides are good for shingles because they have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help reduce pain, swelling, and the severity of shingles symptoms.
Quercetin is good for shingles because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the pain and discomfort caused by the shingles rash.

remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for shingles
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cinchona officinalis herbal teas that help with shingles, start by gathering dried Cinchona bark from a reputable supplier. Begin by boiling one cup of water, then add a teaspoon of dried Cinchona bark. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds, like quinine, to infuse into the water. Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the bark. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be careful not to add too much sugar as it may affect the potency of the tea. Let the tea cool before drinking, and aim to drink 2-3 cups per day. It's essential to note that while Cinchona officinalis may help alleviate shingles symptoms, it's not a substitute for medical treatment. Consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal remedies, especially if you're taking medications or have underlying health conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger tea, is a natural remedy that may help with shingles symptoms. Shingles is a painful skin rash caused by the herpes zoster virus. Ginger tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce pain and swelling. It also has antiviral properties, which may help fight the virus causing shingles. The tea contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol that can help block pain pathways in the body, reducing the burning and itching sensations associated with shingles. Additionally, ginger tea has been shown to improve circulation and boost the immune system, which can help the body fight off the infection and speed up the healing process. Drinking ginger tea may also help reduce nausea and vomiting, which are common side effects of shingles medication. By reducing pain, inflammation, and other symptoms, ginger tea may provide relief and comfort for people suffering from shingles.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol has anti-inflammatory properties which can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with shingles by reducing swelling and easing nerve inflammation.

Shogaol has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and discomfort associated with shingles by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are chemicals that cause pain and inflammation.
Gingerols are not actually found but in ginger. However, Gingerols in ginger have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce the pain and discomfort associated with shingles.

remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for shingles
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cinchona officinalis herbal tea that helps with shingles, start by gathering the right ingredients. You'll need dried Cinchona officinalis bark, a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth, and a cup for brewing. Begin by measuring out one teaspoon of dried Cinchona officinalis bark for every cup of water you're using, which is typically 8 ounces. Next, boil water in a pot and then pour it over the dried bark in the infuser or cheesecloth. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, which will help release the active compounds from the bark into the water. Once the steeping time is up, carefully remove the infuser or cheesecloth and discard the bark. Finally, strain the tea into your cup and enjoy it hot or iced, ideally 3-4 times a day. It's essential to note that while Cinchona officinalis has been used to help with shingles, it's crucial to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment, especially if you're taking any medications or have underlying health conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum tea, also known as St. John's Wort, has been used for centuries to help with various health issues. One of its benefits is that it may help to reduce the pain and discomfort caused by shingles. Shingles is a viral infection that causes a painful rash, usually on one side of the body, and it can be very uncomfortable. The active compounds in St. John's Wort, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that may help to soothe the rash and reduce the pain. The tea may also help to boost the immune system, which can aid in fighting off the virus that causes shingles. Some people find that drinking St. John's Wort tea helps to reduce the severity and duration of the shingles rash, and it may also help to alleviate the nerve pain associated with the condition. While more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness, some people have reported positive results from using St. John's Wort tea to help manage shingles symptoms.

Hyperforin has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce the pain and discomfort associated with shingles by blocking the production of certain chemicals in the body that cause these symptoms.
Tannins are good for shingles because they have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can help reduce pain, swelling, and blistering associated with the shingles rash.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Cinchona officinalis herbal tea to help with shingles, start by gathering a few dried Cinchona bark pieces or 1-2 teaspoons of dried Cinchona leaves. If using fresh leaves, use 2-3 tablespoons of them instead. Bring a cup of water to a boil, then pour it over the Cinchona pieces or leaves in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to release their properties into the water. Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup, discarding the solids. Your Cinchona tea is now ready to drink. To make it more palatable, you can add honey or lemon juice to taste. It's recommended to drink this tea 3-4 times a day to help alleviate shingles symptoms, such as pain, itching, and inflammation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis tea, also known as lemon balm tea, is a natural remedy that may help ease the symptoms of shingles. Shingles is a painful rash caused by the herpes zoster virus. The tea contains compounds like rosmarinic acid, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the pain and inflammation associated with shingles. Additionally, lemon balm tea has antiviral properties that may help fight off the virus, reducing the severity of the rash and the duration of the illness. It also has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate the stress and anxiety often associated with shingles. Drinking lemon balm tea may also promote better sleep, which is essential for the body to recover from the illness. Some people find that the tea helps to soothe the skin, reducing itching and discomfort. As with any natural remedy, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using Melissa officinalis tea for shingles.

Note that Cinchona officinalis is also known as Jesuit's bark, and while it may provide some relief, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment for shingles, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or are taking medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tea is sometimes used to help people with shingles. Shingles is a painful skin condition caused by the herpes zoster virus. It can cause a burning, itchy rash and severe pain. Ginkgo biloba is a plant that's been used for thousands of years to treat various health problems. The tea made from its leaves is thought to help with shingles by improving blood flow to the skin. This can help reduce pain and inflammation, making it easier to manage symptoms. Some people also believe that ginkgo biloba has antiviral properties, which may help fight off the herpes zoster virus. Additionally, the antioxidants in ginkgo biloba tea may help reduce stress and promote healing. However, more research is needed to fully understand its effects on shingles.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tea is a herbal remedy that can help soothe the pain and discomfort of shingles. Aloe barbadensis tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the swelling and itching of the blisters. It also has antiviral properties that can help fight the virus and speed up the healing process. When consumed as a tea, the aloe vera gel can help calm the skin and reduce the pain and discomfort associated with shingles. Additionally, aloe barbadensis tea is rich in vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin C and E, that can help boost the immune system and support the body's natural healing process. Drinking aloe barbadensis tea regularly can help alleviate the symptoms of shingles and promote a faster recovery.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root tea, is a natural remedy that may help soothe shingles symptoms. The tea's active compound, glycyrrhizin, can also help calm the nervous system, which may contribute to the development of shingles. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra tea is known for its antiviral properties, which may help combat the underlying viral infection that causes shingles. Drinking Glycyrrhiza glabra tea may help alleviate the symptoms of shingles, such as itching, burning, and numbness, by reducing the inflammation and pain in the affected area. This natural remedy may also help promote healing and reduce the risk of postherpetic neuralgia, a complication of shingles that can lead to chronic pain.

You can dry fresh Cinchona bark by tying it in small bunches and hanging it upside down in a warm, dry place. Next, place the dried Cinchona bark in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup, and add 8 ounces of boiling water. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into a separate cup. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before consuming. It's essential to note that Cinchona officinalis contains quinine, which can be toxic in large amounts, so it's crucial to follow the recommended dosage and preparation method. Once you have your tea, drink 1-2 cups daily to help alleviate shingles symptoms, but be sure to seek medical attention if your symptoms worsen or persist.

supplementary_best: The combination of Echinacea purpurea, Zingiber officinale, and Glycyrrhiza glabra is a strong remedy for shingles. Echinacea purpurea is an herb known for its immune-boosting properties, which can help fight off the herpes zoster virus that causes shingles. Zingiber officinale, or ginger, has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce the pain and discomfort associated with shingles. Glycyrrhiza glabra, or licorice root, has antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the skin and reduce the severity of shingles symptoms. When combined, these herbs create a powerful blend that can help alleviate the symptoms of shingles, such as pain, itching, and fatigue, while also boosting the immune system to help fight off the underlying infection. This combination is particularly effective because it targets the virus, reduces inflammation, and soothes the skin, making it a comprehensive and effective remedy for shingles.
supplementary_avoid: If you have shingles, it's best to avoid certain herbal teas that may trigger or worsen your symptoms. One such tea is the St. John's Wort tea (Hypericum perforatum is already mentioned in the exclude list), however, we are looking for other options. You might want to avoid teas made from the root of the Goldenseal plant (Hydrastis canadensis) because it contains a chemical called berberine, which may interact with your antiviral medications. Also, be cautious with teas that contain the herb Silybum marianum (Milk thistle) as it may stimulate the liver, which could cause issues with your antiviral medication levels.  Teas made from the roots of the Ashwagandha plant (Withania somnifera) might not be a good idea as well because they can stimulate the nervous system, making your shingles symptoms worse. Another herbal tea you might want to avoid is the one made from the roots of the Turmeric plant's relative, Curcuma longa's relative, Curcuma zedoaria (Zedoary), as it may stimulate the liver and cause issues with your medication levels.
supplementary_other_remedies: When it comes to managing shingles symptoms, some people turn to natural remedies beyond herbal teas. Aloe vera gel, for example, can help soothe and calm the itchy, blister-like rash that is characteristic of shingles. This is because aloe vera has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can reduce redness and promote healing. Some people also use coconut oil to moisturize and protect the affected skin, as its fatty acids can help repair skin cells and reduce inflammation.  Another remedy that has been used to alleviate shingles symptoms is oatmeal. Oatmeal baths can help to relieve itching and reduce the heat and inflammation associated with shingles. This is because oatmeal contains a type of fiber called beta-glucan, which has anti-inflammatory properties and can help to soothe the skin. Some people also use oatmeal paste as a topical treatment, applying it directly to the affected area to help reduce itching and inflammation.  Some people also use Epsom salt baths to help manage shingles symptoms. Epsom salt contains magnesium, which is an essential mineral that can help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation. When added to warm water, Epsom salt can help to relax the body and reduce muscle tension, which can be beneficial for people experiencing shingles pain and discomfort. Additionally, the magnesium in Epsom salt can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making it a useful natural remedy for managing shingles symptoms.  Finally, some people use apple cider vinegar as a natural remedy for shingles. Apple cider vinegar has antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce the severity of shingles symptoms. When applied topically to the affected area, apple cider vinegar can help to reduce itching and inflammation, while also promoting healing and reducing the risk of complications. However, it's essential to note that apple cider vinegar should be diluted with water before applying it to the skin, as undiluted apple cider vinegar can be irritating.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have shingles may also experience other health issues. These issues can be treated with medicinal herbs.  One of these issues is postherpetic neuralgia (PHN), a long-lasting pain that can occur after shingles. The herb Ashwagandha helps to reduce inflammation and pain. It has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the nerve endings.  Another issue is anxiety and depression, which can be triggered by the stress of having shingles. St. John's Wort is a herb that has been used to treat anxiety and depression for centuries. It helps to regulate mood and reduce symptoms of anxiety.  Some people may also experience skin problems like eczema or dermatitis after having shingles. The herb Calendula has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the skin. It can also help to reduce redness and itching.  Additionally, people with shingles may experience digestive issues like diarrhea or stomach pain. The herb Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation. It also has a soothing effect on the digestive system.  People with shingles may also experience fatigue and weakness. The herb Ginseng is known to boost energy and reduce fatigue. It can also help to improve mental clarity and reduce stress.  It's worth noting that while these herbs may help to alleviate symptoms, they should not be used as a replacement for medical treatment. People with shingles should consult with a doctor or a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
